description: Code health pass 1: extract shared test fixtures, deduplicate backend test scenarios, refactor reducer internals, reduce frontend prop drilling, extract shared modal shell, and convert source-string tests to behavioral tests.

userStories.title: Extract shared backend test fixtures into conftest.py
userStories.description: As a developer, I want common test helpers (_make_card, _mock_caller_id, client fixture, make_state, mock_rng) to live in tests/conftest.py instead of being copy-pasted across 12+ test files — so adding a new test file doesn't require duplicating 40 lines of boilerplate.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `tests/conftest.py` with the following shared fixtures/helpers:
`_make_card(idx: int)` — returns a CardDefinition with `card_key=f'card_{idx}'`, tier=1, rarity=15, sides all 4 (w=3), element='shadow'. This is the 'standard' test card used by 11+ files
`_mock_caller_id(request)` — extracts X-User-Id header, defaults to 'test-user'

`client` pytest fixture — creates a TestClient with MemoryGameStore, MemoryCardStore (seeded with `_TEST_CARDS` from 10 `_make_card` calls), and dependency overrides for `get_caller_id` and both stores. Returns the TestClient

`make_state(overrides)` — returns a GameState with sensible defaults (2 players, empty board, version 0). Accepts keyword overrides for any field. The archetype test files (test_caster, test_devout, etc.) should call `make_state(p0_archetype=Archetype.CASTER)` instead of defining their own variant
`mock_rng(*rolls)` — returns a MagicMock with `randint.side_effect = list(rolls)`

Remove the duplicated definitions from all test files that now import from conftest: test_api.py, test_auth_api.py, test_error_responses.py, test_gm011_open_games.py, test_gm013_one_active_game.py, test_idempotency.py, test_leave_game.py, test_ux002.py, test_ux003.py, test_ux004.py, test_ux008.py, test_ux011.py, test_caster.py, test_devout.py, test_intimidate.py, test_skulker.py, test_martial.py, test_captures.py, test_mists.py, test_turn_flow.py

Files with genuinely different `_make_card` signatures (test_cards_balance.py, test_gm001_weak_side.py, test_seed_cards.py) may keep their local variants if the signatures differ materially — but add a comment explaining why
Conftest fixtures must use `@pytest.fixture` properly — `client` is session-or-function scoped, helpers like `_make_card` are plain functions (not fixtures)
`uv run pytest` passes with all existing tests unchanged in behavior
userStories.notes: This is the highest-impact cleanup. The `_make_card` + `_mock_caller_id` + `client` trio is copy-pasted across 12 files. The `make_state` factory has 5 archetype-specific copies that differ only in the default archetype value. The `mock_rng` helper is identical in 3 files. Pytest auto-discovers conftest.py — no explicit imports needed for fixtures. Plain helper functions (not fixtures) DO need explicit imports. Keep test_store.py and test_supabase_store.py untouched if their make_state variants have materially different shapes.

userStories.title: Extract shared frontend test fixtures into helpers.ts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want common frontend test setup (Supabase mock, API mock, MOCK_SESSION, setupAuth, makeGame, mockChannel) to live in web/src/__tests__/helpers.ts instead of being copy-pasted across 20 test files — so test files are focused on assertions, not boilerplate.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `web/src/__tests__/helpers.ts` with the following shared exports:
`MOCK_SESSION` — the standard `{ user: { id: 'user-123', email: 'test@example.com' }, access_token: 'tok' }` object
`MOCK_CARD_DEFS` — an empty Map (or a small populated one if tests need it) for `mockGetCardDefinitions`

`makeGame(overrides?: Partial<GameState>)` — returns a default GameState with game_id, status='active', 2 players, empty board, board_elements, state_version, round_number, result, created_at. Accepts partial overrides
`makePlayer(overrides?: Partial<PlayerState>)` — returns a default PlayerState. Useful for constructing custom player arrays

A `setupSupabaseMock()` function or documented pattern for the `vi.hoisted` + `vi.mock('@/lib/supabase')` block that test files can reuse. Note: due to vitest hoisting constraints, the `vi.mock` call itself must remain in each test file, but the mock factory and the hoisted variables can be shared
A `setupApiMock()` function or documented pattern for `vi.mock('@/lib/api')` — same hoisting constraint applies
Refactor at minimum these files to use the shared helpers: routes.test.tsx, gm011.test.tsx, gm013.test.tsx, gm014.test.tsx, gm015.test.tsx, gm016.test.tsx
For files that only need a subset (e.g. ux* files with simpler mocks), refactoring is optional but importing MOCK_SESSION and makeGame from helpers.ts is encouraged where they apply
`bun run test --run` passes with all existing tests unchanged in behavior
userStories.notes: The vi.mock hoisting constraint is the main complexity here. `vi.mock` calls are hoisted to the top of the file by vitest's transform — the factory function must not reference variables from the module scope UNLESS they are created via `vi.hoisted()`. The practical pattern: helpers.ts exports data (MOCK_SESSION, makeGame) and can export factory functions, but each test file still needs its own `vi.mock(...)` call and its own `vi.hoisted()` block for mock function references. The win is deduplicating the data/factories, not the mock wiring. Don't try to be too clever with mock abstractions — keep it simple. The gm01x and routes test files have the most duplication and should be the priority.

userStories.title: Deduplicate backend test scenarios across test_api / test_auth_api / test_error_responses
userStories.description: As a developer, I want each error scenario (404, 403, 409, 422) tested exactly once — not duplicated across test_api.py, test_auth_api.py, and test_error_responses.py — so I can modify error behavior without updating 3 files.
acceptanceCriteria: Identify all duplicate test scenarios between test_api.py, test_auth_api.py, and test_error_responses.py. Known duplicates: GET /games/{id} 404, POST /games/{id}/join 404, archetype non-participant 403, move non-participant 403, wrong turn 422, stale state_version 409
Keep each scenario in exactly ONE file. Recommended split: test_error_responses.py owns all error-code-specific scenarios (it's organized by status code). Remove the duplicate from test_api.py
test_api.py should keep only happy-path and basic flow tests
test_auth_api.py should keep only auth-specific tests (missing token, invalid token, etc.) — not game-logic errors that happen to return 403/404
Do not delete any test FILE — just remove duplicate test functions and update any shared setup that becomes unused
No test coverage should be lost — every scenario that was tested before must still be tested after, just in one place
`uv run pytest` passes
userStories.notes: The 6 known duplicates are all cases where test_api.py tests an error path that test_error_responses.py also tests more systematically. The fix is simple: delete the duplicate test functions from test_api.py. Check that test_error_responses.py covers each scenario with equivalent or better assertions before deleting. If test_api.py has a better assertion for a particular case, move that assertion to test_error_responses.py instead of deleting it.

userStories.title: Extract archetype dispatch from apply_intent reducer
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the archetype dispatch logic extracted from the 189-line apply_intent function into a dedicated function — so the reducer reads as a clear sequence of phases (validate → archetype → place → capture → score) without a 58-line if/elif block inline.
acceptanceCriteria: Extract the archetype dispatch block (lines ~160-217 in reducer.py) into a new function: `_apply_archetype(state, intent, card) -> tuple[card, ArchetypeResult]` or similar signature that returns the modified card and any archetype side effects (caster_reroll, devout_negate_fog, intimidate target info)
The extracted function should contain all archetype validation and application: MARTIAL rotation, SKULKER boost, CASTER reroll flag, DEVOUT negate flag, INTIMIDATE target validation + debuff info
Keep the function in the same file (reducer.py) — this is an internal refactor, not a new module
The `apply_intent` function should call the extracted function and destructure the result, replacing the inline if/elif block with a single function call + result handling
Define a small dataclass or NamedTuple for the archetype result to avoid returning a loose tuple
Do NOT change any game behavior — this is a pure refactor. All existing tests must pass without modification
`uv run pytest` passes
userStories.notes: The INTIMIDATE branch is the longest (30 lines) because it validates adjacency, checks opponent ownership, etc. The others are 2-6 lines each. The extraction should make apply_intent read like a pipeline: validate turn → apply archetype → place card → resolve captures → check game over → return state. The archetype result type might look like: `ArchetypeResult(card: CardDefinition, caster_reroll: bool, devout_negate_fog: bool, intimidate_debuff: tuple[int, str] | None)`. Keep it simple — don't over-engineer the result type.

userStories.title: Extract adjacency helper into shared board module
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the board adjacency lookup to live in a shared module (app/rules/board.py) instead of being a private dict in captures.py that reducer.py imports via an underscore-prefixed internal reference — so both modules use a clean public API.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `app/rules/board.py` with: the `ADJACENCY` dict (mapping cell index → list of (neighbor_index, attacking_side, defending_side) tuples) and a `get_adjacent_indices(cell_index: int) -> set[int]` helper function
Update `app/rules/captures.py` to import `ADJACENCY` from `app.rules.board` instead of defining `_ADJACENCY` locally
Update `app/rules/reducer.py` to import `get_adjacent_indices` (or `ADJACENCY`) from `app.rules.board` instead of the current `from app.rules.captures import _ADJACENCY` internal import at line ~193
Remove the deferred import inside `apply_intent` — top-level imports only
Add `test_board.py` (or add tests to an existing file) verifying: `get_adjacent_indices(4)` returns {1,3,5,7} (center cell), corner cells return 2 neighbors, edge cells return 3 neighbors
`uv run pytest` passes
userStories.notes: The adjacency data is a static mapping of the 3x3 grid. It's currently defined as `_ADJACENCY` in captures.py and imported (breaking the private convention) by reducer.py. Moving it to a shared board.py is a clean separation. The `get_adjacent_indices` helper is a one-liner (`{nb[0] for nb in ADJACENCY[cell_index]}`) but saves the caller from knowing the tuple structure. Keep board.py minimal — just the adjacency data and helper.

userStories.title: Extract ModalShell shared dialog wrapper component
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a shared <ModalShell> component that handles the backdrop overlay, content container, enter/exit animations, and Escape key handling — so the 4 modal components don't each duplicate 15+ lines of identical wrapper markup.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `web/src/components/ui/ModalShell.tsx` with props: `open: boolean`, `onClose: () => void`, `maxWidth?: string` (default 'max-w-sm'), `className?: string` (for content container), `children: ReactNode`

ModalShell renders: (1) AnimatePresence wrapping the conditional, (2) full-screen backdrop with `bg-black/70`, fade in/out animation, (3) content container with `bg-card border-2 border-border p-6 shadow-xl`, scale+fade animation, (4) Escape key handler calling onClose

Refactor these 4 components to use ModalShell: GameRulesDialog.tsx (maxWidth='max-w-2xl'), ForfeitDialog.tsx (maxWidth='max-w-sm'), ArchetypeModal.tsx (maxWidth='max-w-lg'), CardInspectPreview.tsx (maxWidth='max-w-xs')
Each refactored component should only contain its unique content — title, body, buttons — not the wrapper/animation boilerplate
CardInspectPreview has a dynamic `tierClass` on the container — support this via the `className` prop on ModalShell
GameRulesDialog has a scrollable inner container — this stays inside the dialog content, not in ModalShell
Preserve all existing behavior: close on Escape, backdrop click behavior, animation timing
`bun run test --run` passes
userStories.notes: All 4 modals share identical two-layer motion.div structure (backdrop + content). The only differences are maxWidth and optional extra classes. ModalShell wraps AnimatePresence internally so callers just pass `open` and don't manage AnimatePresence themselves. The Escape handler should be on the backdrop div (or use useEffect with keydown listener). One subtlety: ArchetypeModal currently does NOT have an Escape handler — adding one via ModalShell is fine (it's a UX improvement, not a behavior change). CardInspectPreview currently doesn't wrap itself in AnimatePresence (the caller does) — ModalShell internalizes this.

userStories.title: Reduce ActiveGameView prop count via useGameRoom context
userStories.description: As a developer, I want game room state and callbacks to flow through a React context (useGameRoom) instead of being drilled through 28 props on ActiveGameView — so adding a new piece of game state doesn't require threading a prop through 3 component layers.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `web/src/routes/game-room/GameRoomContext.tsx` with a React context and `useGameRoom()` hook that provides game room state and callbacks

The context should hold at minimum the pass-through props that ActiveGameView never uses directly: `usePower`, `onUsePowerChange`, `powerSide`, `onPowerSideToggle`, `archetypePending`, `archetypeError`, `onSelectArchetype`, `onPreviewCard`, `selectedCardElement`, `onCancelIntimidatePending`, `intimidatePendingCell`
The context provider should be placed in GameRoom.tsx (the parent that currently passes all props to ActiveGameView)
ActiveGameView should consume the context via useGameRoom() for the pass-through values instead of receiving them as props
Child components (ActionPanel, BoardGrid, HandPanel, ArchetypeModal) should consume directly from context instead of receiving props tunneled through ActiveGameView
Props that ActiveGameView uses directly (game, myIndex, myPlayer, myScore, etc.) may remain as props OR move to context — use judgment on what keeps the component readable
ActiveGameView's prop interface should shrink from 28 to ~15 or fewer
No behavior changes — all existing functionality must work identically
`bun run test --run` passes
userStories.notes: The 12-13 pure pass-through props are the clear candidates for context. The remaining ~15 props that ActiveGameView uses directly could stay as props (they document the component's actual interface) or move to context — either is fine as long as the result is cleaner. Don't move everything into context just because you can — if a prop is used by only ActiveGameView and one child, a prop might still be clearer. The test files that render ActiveGameView or GameRoom will need updated setup — they'll need a context provider wrapper. Consider adding a `renderWithGameContext` helper to helpers.ts.

userStories.title: Convert source-string tests to behavioral tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the UXP test files to test rendered behavior (what users see in the DOM) instead of reading .tsx source files with fs.readFileSync and asserting on string content — so tests don't break on every CSS class rename or code reformatting.
acceptanceCriteria: Audit all 19 uxp*.test.tsx files that use `fs.readFileSync` on `.tsx` source files
For each test that reads a .tsx source file and asserts on its content, determine if it can be converted to a behavioral test (render the component, assert on DOM output). Categories:

(a) Tests asserting on CSS classes (e.g. 'contains hover:border-accent') — convert to rendering and checking `getComputedStyle` or checking the element's `className` via `toHaveClass`. If the class is a Tailwind utility that only matters visually, consider whether the test adds value at all
(b) Tests asserting on import statements (e.g. 'imports cardEmoji') — these are implementation tests and should be deleted. The behavior they guard is already covered by the rendered output tests
(c) Tests asserting on structural patterns (e.g. 'uses AnimatePresence') — delete if the animation behavior is tested elsewhere, or convert to a render test that checks the animated behavior

(d) Tests that scan ALL .tsx files for banned patterns (e.g. 'no zinc classes in any file') — these are linting-style checks. Convert to a ruff/eslint rule if possible, or keep as-is since they scan config-like content. These are acceptable
It is acceptable to DELETE source-string tests that have no meaningful behavioral equivalent — not every implementation detail needs a test
Do NOT create new tests that just replicate the source-string check in a different way. If the behavior isn't worth testing via render, delete the test
Tests that read static config files (package.json, index.html, index.css) are OK and should NOT be converted
`bun run test --run` passes
userStories.notes: This is the largest story by file count (19 files) but many of the individual test changes are deletions. The key judgment call: does this test guard behavior a user would notice, or does it guard an implementation detail? 'Button has hover:border-accent' is implementation — if we change the hover color, the test breaks but nothing is actually wrong. 'Card shows emoji for strahd_von_zarovich' is behavior — worth testing. Err on the side of deleting low-value tests rather than investing effort converting them. The goal is fewer, better tests — not the same number of tests in a different style. Work through the files in numeric order (uxp004, uxp006, uxp007, ...). This story may be split if it's too large for one iteration — if so, do the highest-value conversions first and mark the remainder for a follow-up.
